Imagine being 10 years old, working in fields from sunrise to sunset. Your parents tell you education is your only way out. But every school you try rejects you. José Hernández faced this 11 times before NASA finally said yes. At 12 years old, he didn't speak English. His family moved from town to town, following the harvest. Most people said migrant children couldn't become astronauts. But José had a dream. He watched the Apollo 17 mission on television and knew he wanted to go to space. This is the story of how a migrant farmworker became a NASA astronaut.
